description: Give users one clean Settings surface for Advanced Features while removing the obsolete Phase 5 Interactions menu.
steps: Add a namespaced UserDefaults-backed AdvancedFeaturesSettings model that defaults to disabled, publishes changes, and restores its value after relaunch.
Add a localized Advanced Features toggle to PreferencesView and show account login or logout controls only while the toggle is enabled, without duplicating auth state in SwiftUI.
Remove the Phase 5 Interactions submenu, selectors, and production controller wiring while preserving reusable probe fixtures and every unrelated status menu command.
Verify setting persistence and the disabled, enabled-logged-out, and enabled-logged-in Settings states, then open the menu and confirm the obsolete interaction entry is absent.
Run the settings and menu probes plus the full native Swift source typecheck without changing Windows or Electron files.

description: Run music without login by default and activate authenticated study features only when Advanced Features is enabled.
steps: Add a capability policy owned by SceneCoordinator: music and update services are always available, while authentication, review, todo, and reminder data require Advanced Features.

When disabled, skip Keychain session verification and protected pollers, hide review and todo interaction paths, clear user snapshots or pending mutations, and preserve any saved Keychain session without sending logout.

When enabled, reuse the existing AuthCoordinator, browser login callback, Keychain session, repositories, and polling controllers; do not add native credentials UI or duplicate service logic in Preferences or Window layers.

Verify default disabled launch has no login UI or protected requests, music takeover still works, enabling restores valid sessions or reaches logged-out state, and disabling stops protected work without duplicate timers.
Relaunch through disabled, enabled-logged-out, and enabled-logged-in configurations and record deterministic lifecycle evidence.

description: Show a notch-safe spring-driven square Login Required presentation when an advanced logged-out user clicks the island.
steps: Remove the compact Login label, logo, button, and accessibility action so the notch-covered center remains visually and interactively empty in basic and advanced logged-out compact states.
Add pure reducer intents, derived content, and a dedicated visual state for loginRequired; State files must not read settings, open the browser, or call AuthCoordinator.

Make the expanded visible width equal its height, keep the top-center notch anchor fixed, grow downward through the existing Apple spring and single motion driver, and support reverse retargeting plus Reduce Motion.

Render only the localized four-character Simplified Chinese Login Required message below the notch and route taps into this presentation only when Advanced Features is enabled and auth is logged out; browser login remains in Settings.
Verify square geometry, notch clearance, repeated taps, outside collapse, logged-in behavior, music takeover and return, narrow layouts, and deterministic visual frames.

description: Discover trusted MemoryFlow releases through one login-independent production update service.
steps: Add Sparkle 2 through Swift Package Manager and configure an HTTPS appcast URL plus public EdDSA key from build configuration, keeping private signing material outside the repository.

Create Services/Update protocols, a Sparkle adapter, a custom user driver, and an UpdateCoordinator whose typed states cover idle, checking, available, deferred, downloading, ready, installing, and failed.
Keep Sparkle types and callbacks inside Services/Update, publish state on the main actor, and prevent duplicate checks, downloads, recursive callbacks, or stale session updates.
Use signed local appcast fixtures to verify current, newer, malformed, non-HTTPS, and invalid-signature outcomes without importing island presentation types into the updater.
Run coordinator probes, the native build, and the full Swift source typecheck.

description: Check for updates manually and on schedule while honoring a persistent Later choice.
steps: Add a localized Settings update section driven by UpdateCoordinator with Check for Updates, checking, up-to-date, available, and recoverable-error states independent from login and Advanced Features.
Add one injectable schedule owner that checks shortly after launch and every 24 hours, persists the last successful check, catches up after sleep or wake, and never creates duplicate timers.
Add a persistent prompt policy that defers the same version for four hours after Later, still permits manual checks, and clears stale deferral when a newer or installed version supersedes it.
Verify launch, relaunch, manual retry, offline recovery, 24-hour cadence, sleep or wake, four-hour deferral, newer-version replacement, and app termination with a deterministic clock.
Confirm update checks never start AuthCoordinator or protected API traffic.

description: Present an available update in the reusable square island with Update and Later capsule actions.
steps: Adapt UpdateCoordinator availability into pure reducer intents through SceneCoordinator and define prompt precedence and return state alongside basic, advanced, login-required, app activity, and music presentations.

Reuse the login-required square geometry and shared spring while rendering separate update content with a solid blue Update Capsule and solid gray Later Capsule, no border, and no rounded-rectangle button shape.

Keep both actions below the notch with stable sizing, accessibility labels, keyboard focus, and no clipping; SwiftUI sends commands through the render model and never accesses persistence or Sparkle directly.
Route Later through the prompt policy, collapse through the reverse spring, restore compact or music state, suppress the prompt until eligible, and allow a manual Settings check during deferral.
Verify visual frames, hit regions, colors, capsule geometry, notch clearance, Reduce Motion, repeated callbacks, outside clicks, relaunch deferral, and music takeover or return.

description: Download a selected update while the island shows a blue left spinner and white right percentage.
steps: Route Update through SceneCoordinator to UpdateCoordinator, prevent duplicate activation, collapse the square first, and enter update activity only after Sparkle confirms download start.
Convert expected and received byte callbacks into clamped monotonic progress, support unknown length, throttle redundant updates, reset correctly on retry, and ignore stale callbacks.

Render update progress through the existing left-and-right activity layout with a continuously rotating blue indicator on the left, stable-width white percentage on the right, and an empty notch center.
Keep update download activity above login-required and app activity without adding a second motion driver; provide a visibly active nonrotating fallback under Reduce Motion.
Run a signed controlled download covering setup failure, unknown length, retry, 0 to 100 percent progress, layout stability, duplicate clicks, and restoration of the prior presentation.

description: Install verified updates safely and recover without damaging the current app when updating fails.
steps: Map Sparkle ready, verification, authorization, installation, and relaunch callbacks into UpdateCoordinator and settle the island activity before replacement or relaunch.
Delegate EdDSA verification, temporary-file lifecycle, privileged authorization, atomic replacement, and relaunch to Sparkle instead of implementing app replacement in MemoryFlow code.

Map offline, HTTP, malformed feed, insufficient disk, cancelled authorization, and signature errors into recoverable state, collapse to a stable presentation, and expose retry from Settings without an automatic loop.
Install a signed higher-version fixture and verify relaunch, changed bundle version, no repeated offer, and preserved app launchability after each injected failure.
Verify temporary state, timers, prompt deferral, duplicate operations, and future scheduled checks are correct after success and failure.

description: Publish signed and notarized MemoryFlow Island releases through a secure staged appcast pipeline.
steps: Add a release command that enforces increasing marketing and build versions, archives with Developer ID, submits for notarization, staples the ticket, and packages the app for Sparkle.

Generate an EdDSA-signed HTTPS appcast entry with minimum macOS, release notes, content length, and phased-rollout metadata while keeping credentials and the private update key outside the repository.
Publish the immutable update artifact before atomically publishing the appcast so clients never discover an unavailable download.
Verify Gatekeeper, notarization, appcast signature, downgrade rejection, minimum-system filtering, staged-rollout eligibility, and artifact availability with a release candidate.
Document the release inputs, secret locations, rollback procedure, and evidence commands without placing secrets in generated logs.

description: Prove the complete basic, advanced, login-required, and production update experience on macOS.
steps: Launch with Advanced Features disabled and no session, verify an empty compact notch center, no protected traffic, working music, and login-independent manual and scheduled update checks.

Enable Advanced Features, verify the Login Required square and Settings browser login path, restore authenticated review and todo behavior, then disable advanced mode without deleting the saved session.
Discover a signed newer build, verify Later across relaunch, then choose Update and verify prompt collapse, blue spinner, white percentage, safe installation, and relaunch into the new version.

Exercise invalid signature, offline download, insufficient disk, authorization cancellation, music takeover, sleep or wake, rapid clicks, and app relaunch without duplicate timers or corrupted presentation state.
Record API, appcast, signing, transition, visual, percentage, installed-version, and failure evidence under docs/evidence/mac-island-phase7 without modifying Windows or Electron behavior.
